text: The moment of inertia of a solid sphere about its diameter is:
explanation: Solid sphere (diameter): \( I = \frac{2}{5}MR^2 \). Hollow sphere: \( \frac{2}{3}MR^2 \). Solid disc: \( \frac{1}{2}MR^2 \).
name: MOI of Solid Sphere

explanation: Parallel axis theorem: \( I = I_{cm} + Md^2 \), where \(d\) = distance from CM axis to new parallel axis.
name: Parallel Axis Theorem

text: The rotational analogue of Newton's second law is:
explanation: Torque = moment of inertia × angular acceleration: \( \vec{\tau} = I\vec{\alpha} \). Analogous to \( F = ma \).
name: Rotational Newton's 2nd Law

text: Angular momentum \(L\) of a rigid body is:
explanation: Angular momentum: \( L = I\omega \). Also \( L = mvr \) for a point mass. Units: kg m²/s.
name: Angular Momentum

text: Conservation of angular momentum: when a spinning ice skater pulls in arms, angular velocity:
options: Increases (I decreases, so ω increases to keep L constant)
explanation: \( L = I\omega = \text{const} \). Pulling arms in reduces \(I\), so \(\omega\) must increase. Classic example of angular momentum conservation.
name: Ice Skater Angular Momentum

text: The kinetic energy of rolling without slipping is:
explanation: Rolling KE = translational + rotational: \( KE = \frac{1}{2}mv^2 + \frac{1}{2}I\omega^2 \). For solid sphere: \( KE = \frac{7}{10}mv^2 \).
name: Rolling Kinetic Energy

explanation: Torque magnitude: \( \tau = rF\sin\theta \), where \(\theta\) is angle between \(\vec{r}\) and \(\vec{F}\). Maximum when \(\theta = 90°\).
name: Torque Magnitude

text: The perpendicular axis theorem (for lamina in x-y plane) states:
explanation: Perpendicular axis theorem (lamina only): \( I_z = I_x + I_y \), where z-axis is perpendicular to the plane of lamina.
name: Perpendicular Axis Theorem

text: The moment of inertia of a thin rod about an axis through its centre perpendicular to its length is:
explanation: Thin rod (centre, perpendicular): \( I = \frac{ML^2}{12} \). About one end: \( I = \frac{ML^2}{3} \).
name: MOI of Thin Rod

text: The angular velocity \(\omega\) is related to linear velocity \(v\) and radius \(r\) by:
explanation: \( v = r\omega \). Tangential velocity of a point at radius \(r\) from axis rotating at angular velocity \(\omega\).
name: v and ω Relation

text: A torque of 10 N·m acts on a body with moment of inertia 2 kg·m². Its angular acceleration is:
name: Angular Acceleration Calculation

text: The work done by a torque \(\tau\) through angular displacement \(\theta\) is:
explanation: Rotational work: \( W = \tau\theta \). Analogous to linear work \( W = Fs \).
name: Work by Torque

text: The radius of gyration \(k\) is defined by:
explanation: Radius of gyration: \( k = \sqrt{I/M} \), so \( I = Mk^2 \). It represents the effective distance of the mass from the axis.
name: Radius of Gyration

text: For a rigid body in equilibrium, the conditions are:
explanation: Rigid body equilibrium: (1) \( \sum\vec{F} = 0 \) (translational), (2) \( \sum\vec{\tau} = 0 \) (rotational). Both must be satisfied.
name: Conditions for Rigid Body Equilibrium

text: The moment of inertia of a ring about its diameter is:
explanation: Ring (axis through centre perpendicular): \( I = MR^2 \). Ring (diameter): \( I = \frac{MR^2}{2} \) (by perpendicular axis theorem: \( MR^2 = I_x + I_y = 2I_{diameter} \)).
name: MOI of Ring about Diameter
